zh.Gigha, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United: 英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉加
zh.lifeSummary: Niall MacNeill, 5th of Barra, 25th Chief是Muirceartach MacNeil 4th of Barra, 24th Chief的兒子，於大約1220年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉加。他至少有2個兒子。他於1263年登記服兵役。他去世於大約1270年享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: MacNeill,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McNeil
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Néill, a patronymic from the personal name Niall (genitive Néill), see Neil and also Neilson, which was sometimes substituted for McNeil in Lowland Scotland.

History: The Scottish Clan McNeil claims descent from the early Irish king Niall of the Nine Hostages (see   O'Neill  ), and the name is associated in particular with the island of Barra in the Hebrides, having been brought there from Ireland in 1049. Some members of this clan later migrated to Antrim and Derry in Ireland. A different Scottish Clan MacNeill (spelled with two l's) has a traditional ancestral home on the island of Gigha.

nameMeanings.name: Niall
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of disputed derivation; it may mean ‘cloud’, ‘passionate’, or perhaps ‘champion’. It was adopted by the Scandinavians in the form Njal and soon became very popular among them. From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, it has been strongly revived among non-Gaelic speakers since the latter part of the 20th century.

variants.name: Neale
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

variants.name: Neil
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

en.lifeSummary: Niall MacNeill, 5th of Barra, 25th Chief was born about 1220, in Gigha, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom as the son of Muirceartach MacNeil 4th of Barra, 24th Chief. He had at least 2 sons. He registered for military service in 1263. He died about 1270, at the age of 52.

Il a eu au moins 2 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1263. Il est décédé environ 1270, à l’âge de 52 ans.

Er hatte mindestens 2 Söhne. Er hat sich 1263 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb um 1270 im Alter von 52 Jahren.

ko.Gigha, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 지그하
ko.lifeSummary: Niall MacNeill, 5th of Barra, 25th Chief(은)는 대략 1220년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 지그하에서 Muirceartach MacNeil 4th of Barra, 24th Chief의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1263년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 대략 1270년 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

Его отца звали Muirceartach MacNeil 4th of Barra, 24th Chief. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына. В 1263 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер около 1270 г. в возрасте 52 лет.
